function.content:
---
record_type: outbound_email
created_at: 2026-03-31T16:32:00Z
send_state: draft
to:
  - nina@finance-workflow.example
subject: Resending invoice INV-0003 (2025-12-30)
attachments:
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_12_30__eur_000530__inv_0003__northstar_backfill_gamma.md
---
Hi Nina,

Sure — I’m resending the invoice from 2025-12-30 (Nordstern Finanzsoftware GmbH) as requested.

Best,
Miles

next_step.current_state: Outbound record written once as required. Verification: attachment path was previously read, ordering is trivially newest-first with single item, recipient matches Nina canonical email; no rewrite allowed. Next complete inbox workflow by deleting processed file.

next_step.current_state: Inbox request processed end-to-end: verified sender identity canonically, verified requested invoice exists, created outbound draft with exact attachment, and deleted processed inbox file.

function.message: Done — I processed the next inbox message by preparing an outbound email draft to Nina with the requested 2025-12-30 invoice attached, then removed the processed inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed bootstrap and local AGENTS docs, processed lowest inbox filename, used inbox-email header as canonical transport metadata, verified sender against cast primary_contact_email, verified source invoice before sending, wrote one outbox record, then deleted processed inbox file.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox email sender `nina@finance-workflow.example` matched canonical `primary_contact_email` in `10_entities/cast/nina.md` (no sender mismatch), and the requested invoice dated 2025-12-30 from Nordstern Finanzsoftware GmbH was found and read at `50_finance/invoices/2025_12_30__eur_000530__inv_0003__northstar_backfill_gamma.md`. I created `60_outbox/outbox/eml_2026-03-31T16-32-00Z.md` with that exact attachment and then deleted the inbox request per workflow.
